item.title: Summer Soccer Celebration
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 12 AM

In celebration of the world’s greatest soccer tournament playing out in Houston and cities across North America, Levy Park is getting into the festivities with free game watch parties. This series of 15 curated, family-friendly watch parties offers a central gathering place for fùtbol fans to catch some of the tournament’s most anticipated games, including key matchups featuring the United States and Mexico, as well as pivotal knockout rounds. Each free viewing event will feature a large-scale outdoor LED screen with professional sound, entertainment, food and beverage concessions, and merchandise sales. Community Partners: Further enhancing programming, Levy Park will partner with local organizations to broaden community engagement, including collaborations with cultural groups and soccer-focused nonprofits. Levy Park is seeking enthusiastic volunteers and community partner organizations to help bring this festival to life. Volunteers will work directly alongside our event team, supporting booth operations and helping ensure every guest has an unforgettable experience. Email us to learn more about volunteer opportunities. Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds. Group Stage Festival Schedule: Thursday, June 11 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – Mexico vs South Africa (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Friday, June 12 from 6:30 p.m. to 11:00 p.m. – USA vs Paraguay (8:00 p.m. Kick Off) Thursday, June 18 from 6:30 p.m. to 11:00 p.m. – Mexico vs South Korea (8:00 p.m. Kick Off) Friday, June 19 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – USA vs Australia (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Wednesday, June 24 from 6:30 p.m. to 11:00 p.m. – Czechia vs Mexico (8:00 p.m. Kick Off) Round of 32 Festival Schedule: Sunday, June 28 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – 2A vs 2B (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Wednesday, July 1 from 5:30 p.m. to 10:30 p.m. – 1D vs 3BEFIJ (7:00 p.m. Kick Off) Round of 16 Festival Schedule: Monday, July 6 from 5:30 p.m. to 10:30 p.m. – W81 vs W82 (7:00 p.m. Kick Off) Quarter-Finals Festival Schedule: Thursday, July 9 from 1:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m. – W89 vs W90 (3:00 p.m. Kick Off) Friday, July 10 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – W93 vs W94 (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Saturday, July 11 from 2:30 p.m. to 11:00 p.m. – W91 vs W92 (4:00 p.m. Kick Off) & W95 vs W96 (8:00 p.m. Kick Off) Semi-Finals Festival Schedule: Tuesday, July 14 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – W97 vs W98 (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Wednesday, July 15 from 12:30 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. – W99 vs W100 (2:00 p.m. Kick Off) Finals Festival Schedule: Saturday, July 18 from 2:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. – Bronze Final (4:00 p.m. Kick Off) Sunday, July 19 from 12:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. – Final (2:00 p.m. Kick Off)

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Kicking It at Hermann Park
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Commons

This summer, Hermann Park Conservancy will welcome the world to Houston with Kicking It at Hermann Park, presented by ExxonMobil—a free, public celebration of culture, community, and the unifying spirit of sport. Featuring a dynamic mix of programming, diverse food offerings, live entertainment, and family-friendly activities, everything will take place in the Commons, the vibrant 26-acre play and gathering space in the southwest corner of the Park. 📍 The Commons at Hermann Park 📅 Weekend and select weekdays from June 12 – July 5 🎟️ Free & open to the public Kicking It at Hermann Park will run on weekends and select weekdays from Friday, June 12, through Sunday, July 5, offering 15 days of engaging experiences for Houstonians and visitors alike during a globally significant moment for the city. The roster of dynamic programming will include: 🎶 Live music & entertainment 🍴 Local food trucks & culinary offerings 🎬 Outdoor movie nights ⚽ Soccer-inspired play & interactive experiences 🌿 Nature-based activities 👨‍👩‍👧 Family-friendly programming 🎭 Cultural celebrations highlighting Houston’s diversity Thank you to ExxonMobil for their generous support! +++ DATES & THEMES Stay tuned for more details and a complete programming calendar. Activities are subject to change and more may be added. Visit the Conservancy tent on-site in the Commons for any questions! WEEKEND ONE (JUNE 12 – JUNE 14) PDF SCHEDULE HERE. Friday, June 12 (5 – 9 p.m.): Kickoff in the Park 5 – 6 PM: Trees for Texas: A Backyard Tree Walk (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 7 PM: Carousel Karaoke (Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Seeds for Success: The Art of Starting a Garden (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 8 PM: Perennials in the Park: Garden Discovery Tour (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 7 – 9 PM: Open Decks: Live DJ Experience (Powell Foundation Terrace – between Water Play Area and Space Adventure in the Commons) Saturday, June 13 (3 – 10 p.m.): Texas Heritage 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 3 – 4 PM: T is for Texas! Twanda’s Theatre on Wheels from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 3 – 4 PM: Hidden Invaders: Understanding Invasive Species (Next to Lott Family Carousel – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Rhythms of Tradition: Mixteco Ballet Folklorico from Young Audiences of Houston (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 4 – 5 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 -6 PM: Perennials in the Park Tour (McGovern Centennial Gardens) 5 – 8 PM: Hooked on Fishing! Youth Fishing with 4-H (Fishing Pier – The Commons) 5 – 8 PM: DJ Areal: Music in the Commons (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 8:30 PM: Moonlight Movie Nights presented by Goya: The Parent Trap (Doreen Stoller Lawn – The Commons) Sunday, June 14 (3 – 9 p.m.): Family & Global Community Day 3 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 4 – 5 PM: A Walk Through Tranquility: Japanese Garden Tour (Japanese Garden) 5 PM: “One Wan”: Japanese Garden Tea Ceremony (Japanese Garden) 5 – 6 PM: Mosaic Workshop from Young Audiences of Houston (Astros Foundation Pavilion – The Commons) 5 – 6 PM: Bats-a-Billion: Discover the World of Bats (Cullen Pavilion – The Commons) 6 – 7 PM: Winding Paths of the Japanese Garden: Guided Tour (Japanese Garden) 7 – 9 PM: Jazz in the Park with Bayou City Jazz Band (Tsuru Family Terrace – The Commons) Wednesday, June 17 (3 – 9 p.m.): Wellness in the Park – THIS DATE HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO INCLEMENT WEATHER! Friday, June 19 (5 – 9 p.m.): Juneteenth Celebration Saturday, June 20 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global Rhythms Sunday, June 21 (3 – 9 p.m.): Community & Wellness Day Tuesday, June 23 (3 – 9 p.m.): Youth + Play + Sustainability Day Friday, June 26 (5 – 9 p.m.): Latin Night Saturday, June 27 (3 – 10 p.m.): Global DJ Night Sunday, June 28 (3 – 9 p.m.): International Culture Day Monday, June 29 (3 – 9 p.m.): Arts + Nature Day Friday, July 3 (5 – 10 p.m.): Summer Nights in the Park Saturday, July 4 (3 – 7 p.m.): Celebrate America – Houston Style Sunday, July 5 (3 – 9 p.m.): Closing Celebration +++ MOONLIGHT MOVIE NIGHTS Presented by Goya As part of the summer offerings, enjoy Moonlight Movie Nights, presented by Goya, in the Commons! Movies will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. These movies are free and open to the public. Saturday, June 13: The Parent Trap (1998; PG) Saturday, June 20: A Bug’s Life (1998; G) Saturday, June 27: Lilo & Stitch (2002; PG) Friday, July 3: National Treasure (2004; PG) Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. +++ To inquire about sponsorship opportunities and ways to get involved, email community@hermannpark.org.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Wellness Walk (CANCELED DUE TO WEATHER)
item.description:
Tuesday, June 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: McGovern Centennial Gardens, 1500 Hermann Drive, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

THIS EVENT HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO THE WEATHER. Join Laura Conely of Urban Paths for a fun evening as we explore the trails and beauty of Hermann Park on a guided 3-mile experience. All are invited to this conversationally paced walk; friendly pups on leash are welcome too! This event is free, but registration is required and space is limited. Sign up HERE. Participants will meet outside the McGovern Centennial Gardens, located at 1500 Hermann Drive, with parking available in adjacent Lot C. Laura Conely is the founder of Urban Paths, a walking wellness company. As a certified teacher and coach, she leads guided Wellness Walks that bring individuals of all ages and fitness levels together to experience the profound health benefits of: Nature, Art, Movement, and Connection.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Texas Sound Meditation with Leala, Melissa, and James
item.description:
Tuesday, June 16, 2026 at 7 PM

Levy Park brings “Self Care” to a whole new level with Sound Healing led by Leala, Melissa, and James. A sound bath is a gentle, nurturing, and cathartic meditation experience for anyone looking to improve the quality of their life. This act of self-care is beneficial for anyone from beginners to advanced meditators who are ready to experience a greater sense of peace and well-being. The Levy Park Sound Baths will assist in clearing out that which is blocking the natural vitality while strengthening the connection and balance between mind, body, and soul. A collectively cultivated safe space offers participants an opportunity to deepen their own journey of self-love, healing or transformation. While relieving stress, tension and anxiety this sound immersion experience will have you feeling revitalized, relaxed and rejuvenate. Let the sounds wash away everything that no longer serves you as you set your intentions and manifest your desires!  

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park (CANCELED DUE TO WEATHER)
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

THIS EVENT HAS BEEN CANCELED DUE TO THE WEATHER. Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: BATTLE OF THE BADGES
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 2 PM

? BATTLE OF THE BADGES — JUNE 30, 2:00PM–8:00PM Come donate blood and cast your vote — HPD or HFD, who will win? The Battle of the Badges blood drive takes place Tuesday, June 30 from 2:00–8:00pm at the Historic Houston Heights Fire Station (107 W. 12th St) as part of the city-wide competition running June 28–July 5. Every successful donor receives a special edition baseball cap and a FREE $25 Donor Rewards Store gift card! Use sponsor code HHA8 when scheduling. Click below to sign up and help your team win!   https://www.commitforlife.org/donor/schedules/drive_schedule/416139

Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

item.title: Moonlight Movie Nights: National Treasure
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: The Commons

As part of this summer’s Kicking It at Hermann Park offerings, enjoy “Moonlight Movie Nights,” presented by Goya, in the Commons! On Friday, July 3, join us for a showing of National Treasure (2004 | PG | 2h 10m runtime). The movie will start at approximately 8:30 p.m., around sunset. The movie showing is free and open to the public. Be sure to get to the Commons early to snag your spot on the lawn. Guests are welcome to bring blankets or lawn chairs to settle in for the movie. Food and beverage will be available for purchase at the cafe and with food vendors on-site. Thank you to Goya for their generous support!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Introduction to Teaching the Holocaust
item.description:
Tuesday, July 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Holocaust Museum Houston Classroom

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a one day workshop designed to help educators to feel comfortable teaching about the Holocaust. The day will include a tour of the museum’s Holocaust Gallery, a talk from a Holocaust survivor or descendant, best practices for teaching about the Holocaust, an overview of some of the free resources available, and more.  Breakfast and lunch will be provided. Teachers will earn 6 CPE hours and 6 GT update hours. Teachers from the 6th-12th grade, in all subject areas, are encouraged to participate in this FREE workshop. Sign up through the registration link below. If you have any questions about the workshop, contact us as education@hmh.org.    Interested in a more in-depth exploration of the Holocaust? We also offer the Max M. Kaplan Summer Institute for Educators, which includes pedagogical and content sessions from scholars and museum educators. You can find more information and apply here: https://hmh.org/education/professional-development/max-m-kaplan-summer-institute/. Register Here

From Holocaust Museum Houston

Event details

item.title: Educating Traumatized Teenagers: The Extraordinary Story of a Swedish International School
item.description:
Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

After World War II, Sweden absorbed and provided medical treatment for thousands of sick survivors. What to do about those who were teenagers, who had missed years of study on account of the war?  In September 1945, the Swedish government issued an order for schooling to be offered to those under the age of 18. In this presentation, Dr. Bernice Lerner will describe the complex process of recruiting wary adolescents, the challenges of teaching homeless, orphaned, and “environmentally damaged” teenagers, and instructors’ goals and methods at the most experimental of sites—the “Internat” school her mother attended. Dr. Bernice Lerner is the author of All the Horrors of War: A Jewish Girl, a British Doctor, and the Liberation of Bergen-Belsen, and other writings on the Holocaust and on virtue ethics. She is the former dean of adult learning at Hebrew College, former lecturer on the Holocaust at Boston University, and a senior scholar at Boston University’s Center for Character and Social Responsibility. Gerald S. Kaplan Endowed Lecture.

From Holocaust Museum Houston

item.title: Author Talk with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon From Chicago
item.description:
Thursday, August 27, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Albert and Ethel Herzstein Theater on the Mady and Ken Kades Stage

Join Holocaust Museum Houston for a night with Dr. Pamela Toler, Author of The Dragon from Chicago: The Untold Story of an American Reporter in Nazi Germany.      “Today we face an alarming upsurge in the spread of misinformation and attempts by powerful figures to discredit facts so they can seize control of our political and cultural narratives. These are threats American journalist Sigrid Schultz knew all too well. The Chicago Tribune’s Berlin bureau chief and primary foreign correspondent for Central Europe from 1925 to January 1941, Schultz witnessed Hitler’s rise to power and was one of the first reporters—male or female—to warn American readers of the growing dangers of Nazism.  She was one of the last to leave Berlin before it was too late.     In The Dragon From Chicago, historian Pamela Toler tells the story of Schultz’s years spent courageously reporting the news from Berlin, from the revolts of 1919 through the Nazi rise to power and Allied air raids over Berlin in 1941. At a time when women reporters rarely wrote front-page stories, Schultz regularly  reported the truth about Nazi Germany in the face of censorship and the threat of expulsion, internment, or death. The Nazis called Schultz “that dragon from Chicago.” One of her fellow correspondents called her “Adolph Hitler’s greatest enemy.” Schultz herself claimed to be “just a reporter.” Her story is a powerful account of one woman standing up for truth in an era marked by the spread of disinformation and propaganda spawned by hate.”     Books will be available for purchase by credit card.

From Holocaust Museum Houston

item.description:
